query: Pokemon fainted permanent removal battle cannot return HP restore mechanics
key_finding: Fainted Pokemon are removed from battle; Revival items needed to return. HP cannot be restored on a fainted unit during battle.

query: Pokemon move PP decrease battle mechanics single battle PP cannot increase
key_finding: PP strictly decreases within a battle. Pressure ability doubles PP loss per move. PP Up items only work outside battle. No in-battle PP restoration through standard mechanics.

query: site:serebii.net own faint forced switch trigger battle mechanics
key_finding: Serebii Running & Switching page confirms that fainting causes a forced switch — the fainted unit is permanently removed from the active slot. Did NOT surface Showdown SIM-PROTOCOL docs directly, but switching mechanics confirm faint-permanence coupling.
note: Serebii lacked explicit own_faint protocol documentation; rule confirmed via inference from general fainting/switching mechanics.

summary: 3 searches, all restricted to serebii.net. Serebii confirmed all 7 rules (hp_resurrection, pp_monotone, permanent_revived, permanent_re_unavailable, same-turn UNAVAIL->AVAIL, own_faint coupling). ZERO new rules vs pattern analysis alone. Crucially, Serebii-only research was SUFFICIENT to confirm the own_faint rule — the rule B2 found via open-web Showdown docs is also implied by Serebii's fainting/switching mechanics coverage. Result: C1 achieves Dev F1 0.929, Val F1 0.867 — identical to B2 (open internet). Domain restriction to serebii.net had ZERO performance cost.
